markdown:
#  What are Procore's default cost types? 
##  Answer 
A _Cost Type_ is a set of costs identified by a unique abbreviation or label. Cost types are typically used in job costing (tracking costs for certain job activities and projects). In Procore's Work Breakdown Structure, 'Cost Type' is a flat segment with these default options: _(E) Equipment, (L) Labor, (M) Materials, (O) Other, (OC) Owner Cost, (S) Commitments,_ and _(SVC) Professional Services._
In Procore, cost types are a way to identify and classify your project's job costs. They can also be used to generate reports. View the Job Cost Summary Report in [View a Report](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/reports-company/tutorials/view-a-report). When managing your cost types list in Procore:
  * Your company's Procore Administrator can add an unlimited number of new segment items to the 'Cost Type' segment in Procore's Work Breakdown Structure (WBS). To add segment items to the Company level, see [Add Segment Items](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/company-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/add-segment-items). To add segment items to a project, see [Add Segment Items to a Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/project-administration-work-breakdown-structure-guide/add-segment-items).
  * If your company is using the ERP Integrations tool, you must assign individual cost type segment items to the individual codes in the 'Cost Code' segment. See [Assign Default Cost Types To Cost Codes](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/assign-default-cost-types-to-erp-standard-cost-codes).


In the construction industry, grouping creating relationships between your project's _cost codes_ and _cost types_ is a common practice.
#####  Note
For companies using Procore's Company level ERP Integrations tool, some [integrated ERP systems](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) refer to Procore's 'cost type' concept using an alternate term. To learn more, see [What are 'cost types' and how does our ERP integration support them?](https://support.procore.com/faq/what-are-cost-types-and-how-does-our-erp-integration-support-them)
### Default Cost Type Segment
This table details the default options in the 'Cost Type' segment in Procore's WBS. Although you cannot delete the default options, your company's Procore Administrator can change the codes and descriptions or add, edit, and delete custom cost type segment items.  
| E  | Equipment  |  
| --- | --- |  
| L  | Labor  |  
| M  | Materials  |  
| O  | Other  |  
| OC  | Owner Cost  |  
| S 1  | Commitment  |  
| SVC  | Professional Services  |  
1 _The "S" is short for_ Subcontract.
##  ERP Integrations 
If your company is using the ERP Integrations tool, learn more about cost types:
  * [What are 'cost types' and how does our ERP integration support them?](https://support.procore.com/faq/what-are-cost-types-and-how-does-our-erp-integration-support-them)
  * [How do I add a new {{integrations_Sage300CRE}} cost code or cost type?](https://support.procore.com/tc/procore/Legacy/ERP%5FArchives/Legacy-Sage300CRE-Tutorials/How%5Fdo%5FI%5Fadd%5Fa%5Fnew%5FSage%5F300%5FCRE%5Fcost%5Fcode%5For%5Fcost%5Ftype)
  * [Can I delete a {{integrations_Sage300CRE}} cost code, cost type, or vendor from Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-can-i-delete-a-sage-300-cre-cost-code-cost-type-or-vendor)
  * [{{integrations_Spectrum}}: Request to Import Custom Cost Types for {{integrations_DexterChaney}}](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/viewpoint-spectrum/tutorials/request-to-import-viewopint-spectrum-cost-types-to-procore)
  * [{{integrations_RyvitVista}}: Assign](https://support.procore.com/tc/procore/Legacy/ERP%5FArchives/Legacy%5FVista%5FTutorials/assign-default-cost-types-to-vista-viewpoint-cost-codes) [Default Cost Types To {{integrations_RyvitViewpoint}} Cost Codes](https://support.procore.com/tc/procore/Legacy/ERP%5FArchives/Legacy%5FVista%5FTutorials/assign-default-cost-types-to-vista-viewpoint-cost-codes)


## See Also
  * [What are Procore's default cost codes?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-default-cost-codes)
  * [What is a budget code in Procore's WBS?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-budget-code-in-procores-wbs)
  * [Which Procore tools support Work Breakdown Structure?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-procore-tools-support-work-breakdown-structure)


A _Procore Administrator_ is a user who has 'Admin' level permissions on all of the Company level Tools in Procore. Granting a user ‘Admin’ level permissions in the Company level Directory tool automatically assigns that user ‘Admin’ permissions on all Company level tools. Also called a _Company Administrator_.
A Subcontract is a legal agreement where a party on a prime contract engages a third-party (the subcontractor) to perform all or part of the work defined in the prime contract.
title: What are Procore's default cost types?
description: Explains what Procore's default cost types are.
